plays.analysis:
Jeffrey Springs owns excellent career numbers against Detroit, but he has been in terrible form with a 9.00 ERA and 13 home runs allowed over his last seven starts.

Troy Melton has recorded six quality starts in seven outings and has been the much more trustworthy starter. The Athletics have also been one of baseball's weakest road offenses against right-handed pitching over the last 30 days.

Detroit has the stronger current form and the better matchup against a struggling starter and a poor Athletics bullpen.

Pittsburgh has scored 12, 11, 7, 5, 6, 6, 0, 11, 9 and 7 runs over its last 10 games. Grant Holmes has delivered seven straight quality starts, but he has allowed eight home runs over that stretch and owns a 4.09 FIP with a 1.36 WHIP.

Jared Jones has allowed five home runs in 29 innings and faces an Atlanta offense that has surged over the last seven games, ranking near the top of the league in average, slugging, OPS, wRC+ and isolated power.

The wind is blowing out at PNC Park, and the conditions are hot and humid. Atlanta should get opportunities against Jones and Pittsburgh's weak bullpen, while the Pirates' current form gives them a chance to score even against Atlanta's elite relief corps.

Colin Rea has been struggling lately, posting a 4.06 ERA over his last seven starts while allowing six home runs. Walks have also become an issue, and Baltimore is excellent at drawing them.

Dean Kremer is making only his third start of the year and has already allowed five home runs. The Cubs match up extremely well with his splitter, sinker and cutter, while their offense is averaging 6.2 runs over its last 10 games.

The wind is blowing slightly out to left and both bullpens are roughly average. The price is juiced, but the matchup still points toward runs.

Burns has recorded 10, 7, 7, 7, 9 and 8 strikeouts in six of his previous seven starts. Philadelphia ranks near the bottom of baseball in strikeout rate, including against right-handed pitching.

The Phillies' success against his pitch mix is the primary risk, but their aggressive approach gives Burns enough opportunities to reach eight strikeouts.

Chase Burns gives Cincinnati a real chance against Philadelphia. He has regularly produced seven to ten strikeouts and has held the Phillies to one run in each of his two previous meetings.

Philadelphia's lineup is dangerous and grades well against Burns' pitch mix, but it also strikes out heavily against right-handed pitching. The uncertainty around Philadelphia's starter creates the opportunity for Cincinnati to manufacture enough early offense.

The Reds' offense is the concern, so the first-five market is preferable to relying on the full game.

Spencer Arrighetti owns a respectable season ERA, but he has posted a 6.57 ERA with nine home runs allowed over his last seven starts. Control has also been a problem, with an 11.5% walk rate.

Washington can attack him with four left-handed hitters and two switch hitters, including James Wood, Luis Garcia and CJ Abrams. Arrighetti has been much weaker against left-handed bats.

Foster Griffin gives Washington the starting-pitching edge, while Houston's bullpen has improved only to roughly average. The Nationals team total is the preferred angle, with a smaller look toward the full-game over.

plays.game: Kansas City Royals at New York Mets
plays.play: New York Mets Team Total Over 4.5
plays.analysis:
The Royals are likely to cover the game with Steven Cruz, Randy Dobnak and a poor bullpen that used five relievers the previous day.

New York has scored 12, 7 and 10 runs over its last three games and faces favorable hitting weather at Citi Field. The alternate 4.5 team total is preferred because the standard number may require too much trust in an inconsistent Mets offense.

plays.game: Kansas City Royals at New York Mets
plays.analysis:
Kansas City is expected to use a bullpen game led by Steven Cruz and Randy Dobnak. Cruz owns a 5.08 ERA and 1.45 WHIP, Dobnak has a 4.59 ERA and 1.75 WHIP, and the Royals bullpen has a 5.22 ERA with a 1.56 WHIP. Five Kansas City relievers worked the previous day.

The Mets have scored 12, 7 and 10 runs in their last three games and rank inside the top 10 offensively over the last week. Kansas City has also hit well recently.

Christian Scott brings strikeout upside but has walked too many hitters and allowed six home runs over his last three starts. Both bullpens are vulnerable and heavily worked, while warm, humid conditions and an 11.4 mph wind blowing out favor offense.

plays.game: Toronto Blue Jays at San Francisco Giants
plays.analysis:
Cease has either kept the walks to one or allowed four or five, and San Francisco has been seeing the ball well over the last 30 days. The Giants rank fifth in walks against right-handed pitching at home during that span.

The season-long walk numbers for San Francisco are less impressive, but Cease has allowed exactly two walks each time he has faced them. At -145, over 1.5 walks is fair for roughly one-third of a unit.
